title: Technology & the Human | NOEMA
gist: This is a category index page (not a full article) from Noema Magazine listing 12 of 227 essays and interviews in the 'Technology & the Human' section. The only substantive sentence describes the section as exploring how AI and biotechnology challenge established conceptions of humanity; the remainder is article titles and author names only.

insights.claim: Eric Schmidt gave an interview to Noema Magazine titled 'AI & Drones: The Biggest Revolution In The History Of Warfare', categorised under Geopolitics & Globalization, framing AI-enabled drones as historically unprecedented in military significance.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: Schmidt's framing — 'biggest revolution in the history of warfare' — signals that prominent technologists view AI-drone convergence as a defense-sector paradigm shift; relevant for defense-AI and drone-hardware investment theses.
insights.quote: AI & Drones: The Biggest Revolution In The History Of Warfare

insights.claim: Nathan Gardels wrote a Noema essay arguing that OpenAI is proposing a 'Social Contract' for the Intelligence Age, framing AI governance as requiring a formal societal compact rather than piecemeal technical regulation.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: OpenAI's social-contract framing suggests the company is positioning for institutional and regulatory legitimacy; investors should watch how this shapes government partnership bids and antitrust outcomes.
insights.quote: OpenAI Proposes A ‘Social Contract’ For The Intelligence Age

insights.claim: Pope Leo has publicly called for AI-generated wealth to be universally shared, per a Noema essay by Nathan Gardels, extending Catholic social teaching explicitly to AI distributional policy.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: Papal endorsement of AI wealth redistribution adds unexpected institutional weight to the redistribution debate; could influence European and Latin American regulatory proposals on AI taxation or profit-sharing.
insights.quote: Pope Leo: AI Wealth Must Be Universally Shared

insights.claim: Nathan Gardels predicted in a Noema essay that the next US presidential election will be defined by AI as the central policy issue, not the economy or immigration.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: If AI dominates a US presidential cycle, AI regulation, compute controls, and sector taxation would gain acute electoral salience — a high-stakes policy risk for large-cap AI companies.
insights.quote: The Next US Presidential Election Will Be About AI

insights.claim: Noema Magazine's 'Technology & the Human' section, spanning 227 articles, defines its editorial mission as exploring how AI and biotechnology challenge established conceptions of what it means to be human.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: The volume and editorial framing signal that AI-and-human-identity is a sustained intellectual preoccupation across philosophy, biotech, and governance circles — context for assessing regulatory sentiment around AI autonomy and biotech personhood.
insights.quote: Exploring the manifold ways in which artificial intelligence and biotechnology challenge our established conceptions of what it means to be human.

insights.claim: Daniele Cavalli wrote for Noema on how to protect human autonomy in an age of AI, indicating that autonomy-preservation is gaining traction as a formal design and policy constraint, not merely a philosophical concern.
insights.speaker: Noema Magazine
insights.so_what: Philosophical discourse on AI and autonomy is feeding into regulatory frameworks; AI platform investors should monitor how autonomy-preservation mandates could reshape product design requirements and compliance costs.
